creator: fabian@wenks.ch
text:
(In reply to Fabian Wenk from comment #12)

It took me a while, but actually it is just in front of our eyes in comment #9. Maybe the easiest solutions for users (forced to) migrate from MariaDB 10.3 to 10.6 would be to adjust the socket in /usr/local/etc/mysql/my.cnf back to '/tmp/mysql.sock'.

I am not saying this is a nice solution, as having the socket in /tmp is not really recommended any more. But sure it is the simplest to not run into all the problems we had seen so far. On the other hand there may arise future difficulties with the old socket path.
